_capture_metadata.purpose: Frozen counts establishing the master recipient list and validating the cohort against the documented filter window.

queries.label: Master count: Contacts currently tagged 'FCA Update Promo'
queries._note: **This is the FC-FCA-01 master recipient list.** Every Contact processed by sequence 2 of workflow rule 794088000139471721 receives this tag. With over_write=false, the tag persists through subsequent campaigns.

queries.label: Sanity check: tagged AND created on or before 16 Oct 2025
queries._note: Identical to master count → 100% of tagged Contacts existed before the send date. No anomalies (e.g. Contacts created after Oct 16 carrying the tag erroneously).

queries.label: Cohort intersection: tagged AND lead-creation in 6–28 Aug 2025
queries._note: Of the 2,818 recipients, 1,848 fall in the strict 6–28 Aug 2025 lead-creation window described in the meeting transcript. The remaining 970 are tagged Contacts created OUTSIDE that window but on/before Oct 16. Implication: Laura's actual cohort selection was wider than 'leads created Aug 6–28'. The tag is more authoritative than any retrospective filter reconstruction — it captures the actual cohort the workflow processed, not what we infer from filter rules.

queries.label: Cross-source confirmation: Paul Aldridge (Desk reply ticket #87471) is in the tagged cohort
queries._note: Independent confirmation. Paul Aldridge replied to the campaign email on 16 Oct 2025 11:07 (Desk ticket #87471). His Contact in CRM has the FCA Update Promo tag. Cross-source agreement — the tag-based reconstruction is consistent with independent Desk-side evidence.

queries.label: Cumulative Email Sent count (NOT campaign-specific — included for completeness)
queries._note: Total Contacts whose Mass_Email_Clients picklist currently reads 'Email Sent'. This is cumulative across ALL 8 campaigns sent via this workflow rule since Oct 2025; the picklist is overwritten on every send. Not useful for FC-FCA-01 reconstruction in isolation — documented here only so the reader understands why field-state alone fails.

_dead_query_documented_for_completeness.label: Direct picklist-value query for the Oct 16 campaign (S1 — plan-doc primary, found dead)
_dead_query_documented_for_completeness._note: Returns zero because the Deluge function overwrites the picklist to 'Email Sent' immediately after a successful send. With history_tracking_enabled=false on the field, no audit record of the Oct 16 value setting survives.
